---
title: Welcome
format: html
date: last-modified
---
Welcome to the Git book for _Flyway Assessment of Offshore Wind Farm Impacts on Migratory Fishes_, a project funded by the [Responsible Offshore Science Alliance](https://www.rosascience.org). Led by scientists in the [TailWinds](https://tailwinds.umces.edu) team at the [University of Maryland Center for Environmental Science](https://www.umces.edu), the project aims to quantify "flyways" of marine fauna in such a way that they can be evaluated for changes resulting from impacts to their migratory corridors. _Flyways_ is a collaborative project between investigators at the University of Delaware, the University of Rhode Island, Delaware State University, and the St. Mary's College of Maryland; however, it is not possible without years of hard work by [those who have tagged and detected fishes for over a decade](#special-thanks). 

Scroll down to skim the fact sheet for the project or watch a presentation from analyst Mike O'Brien.

::: {.callout-warning}
This book is undergoing rapid development and is not peer reviewed. Everything you read should be considered preliminary and subject to substantial change.
:::

# Fact Sheet
![](fact_sheet.jpg)

# Presentation
{{< video  https://youtu.be/bUBgI-nKcWE start="703" >}}

# Special Thanks!
A special thanks to those who have agreed to share data in fulfillment of this project:

- Matt Balazik (Virginia Commonwealth University)
- Ben Gahagan (Massachusetts Division of Marine Fisheries)
- Bill Hoffman (Massachusetts Division of Marine Fisheries)
- Ashlee Horne (Maryland Department of Natural Resources)
- Ian Park (Delaware Division of Fish and Wildlife)
- Chuck Stence (Maryland Department of Natural Resources)
